title: v18 Live Cutover: opt-in dress rehearsal checklist for real browser/API routes
description:
## Objective
Create an opt-in live cutover dress rehearsal for v18 that proves real routes without confusing live readiness with mock success.

## Background / Rationale
Mocks prove behavior, but live routes prove real provider capability, browser evidence, subscriptions, credentials, and operator workflow. The PLAN requires a live-cutover checklist, explicit approvals, and no mock-as-live ambiguity.

## Scope
- Script/checklist that verifies capabilities, provider docs freshness, browser lease/evidence, API credentials, route plan, budget, waiver policy, and operator approvals before live calls.
- Run a tiny controlled live profile or staged smoke according to the checklist.
- Capture evidence/artifact index/run report while redacting secrets and avoiding raw hidden reasoning.
- Clearly label output as live, with provider ids, timestamps, and operator approval ids.

## Logging Requirements
- Logs under `tests/logs/v18/live/` only when explicitly invoked.
- Include checklist id, approval id, provider capability snapshot, route id, evidence ids, artifact paths, and live/mock discriminator.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Live smoke is never run by default CI and cannot be mistaken for a mock pass.
close_reason: Added opt-in v18 live cutover dress rehearsal harness with approval/live-env gates, redacted log bundle, checklist/evidence/artifact validation, and integration coverage

title: v18 E2E: mock fast/balanced/audit profile scripts with full pipeline logging
description:
## Objective
Add black-box mock E2E scripts for the full v18 planning pipeline across fast, balanced, and audit profiles.

## Background / Rationale
System-level bugs often appear only when commands, artifacts, route state, prompts, providers, synthesis, and reports interact. Mock E2E should prove the CLI journey works before any live provider spend.

## Scope
- Create temp project fixtures with README/spec/implementation and `.apr/` planning config.
- Run capabilities/doctor, route plan, readiness, prompt compile/lint, fanout, normalize, compare, synthesize, export-beads dry-run, report, and status.
- Cover fast, balanced, and audit profile differences.
- Verify artifacts: source baseline, prompt context, provider results, evidence refs, Plan IR, comparison, synthesis, traceability, review packet, artifact index, events, and logs.

## Logging Requirements
- Logs under `tests/logs/v18/e2e/<profile>/<timestamp>/`.
- Include full command transcript, stdout/stderr split, env, run id, route ids, provider ids, artifact tree, JSON summaries, and failure rerun command.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A single command can run the v18 mock E2E suite and leave enough logs to debug failures without reproduction.

title: v18 Tests: negative/security regressions for unsafe shortcuts, evidence gaps, and prompt trust failures
description:
## Objective
Add negative and security regression tests that prove v18 refuses unsafe shortcuts and misleading states.

## Background / Rationale
The most damaging bugs are not ordinary failures; they are false-success states where APR appears to complete a high-reasoning planning route using a substituted API, unverified browser state, incomplete search, untrusted prompt text, or incomplete quorum.

## Scope
- Prohibited API substitution for ChatGPT/Gemini/Claude.
- Codex formal first-plan misuse.
- Unverified/low-confidence browser evidence.
- DeepSeek search disabled/missing citation traces.
- TOON/tru treated as authoritative state.
- Circular synthesis readiness or synthesis without quorum/evidence.
- Prompt-injection text escaping quarantine.
- Raw reasoning or secret leakage in artifacts/logs.

## Logging Requirements
- Logs under `tests/logs/v18/negative/` with scenario id, expected failure code, actual failure code, blocked reason, redaction findings, artifact paths, and rerun command.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each unsafe shortcut fails closed with a specific robot/human explanation.
close_reason: Added v18 negative/security fail-closed regression harness covering unsafe shortcuts, redaction leaks, prompt trust failures, and synthesis gate regressions with integration coverage

title: v18 Provider: Oracle browser route lease/evidence integration for ChatGPT Pro and Gemini Deep Think
description:
## Objective
Integrate APR v18 browser-backed routes with Oracle lease/session/evidence artifacts for ChatGPT Pro and Gemini Deep Think.

## Background / Rationale
APR should not own brittle DOM automation. Oracle owns browser interactions; APR owns route planning, prompt payloads, evidence eligibility, and artifact recording. Browser evidence must prove the right model/effort was selected before prompt submission.

## Scope
- Request/acquire browser lease/session information from Oracle-compatible surfaces.
- Always use inline prompt/document paste semantics where APR controls content; preserve the existing `--browser-attachments never` reliability principle.
- Verify evidence before accepting provider result: model label, effort/mode, capture confidence, selector manifest id, prompt/output hashes, and redaction status.
- Surface fallback/manual-import states if browser evidence is missing or confidence is too low.
- Record evidence references in provider results and run artifacts, not raw browser secrets.

## Test Requirements
- Mock Oracle evidence fixtures for success, missing effort, low confidence, stale selector manifest, and unverified evidence.
- Mock E2E must prove synthesis gates reject unverified browser evidence.
- Logs include route id, lease id, session id, evidence id, selector manifest id, confidence, prompt hash, and redaction verdict.

## Acceptance Criteria
- ChatGPT/Gemini browser results are accepted only with eligible evidence or explicit degraded/manual waiver path.
close_reason: Added browser evidence linkage conformance checker for lease/session/evidence/provider-result eligibility and negative evidence states

title: v18 Contracts: plan artifact, traceability, artifact index, and approval ledger
description:
## Objective
Define the contracts for final and intermediate planning artifacts, traceability, artifact indexing, and approvals.

## Background / Rationale
The synthesis output must be explainable: users should know which provider/source/evidence supported each recommendation, contradiction, acceptance criterion, test, and rollback point. Artifact indexes and approvals make long runs auditable.

## Scope
- Implement/land schemas for plan artifact, traceability, artifact index, and approval ledger.
- Represent Plan IR stages, provider result references, source baseline hashes, evidence refs, reviewer deltas, contradiction/resolution records, acceptance criteria, test matrix, rollback points, and human review decisions.
- Ensure artifact paths are relative, portable, and safe for repo-local archival.

## Test Requirements
- Positive fixtures `plan-artifact.json`, `traceability.json`, `artifact-index.json`, and `approval-ledger.json` validate.
- Tests verify every final plan item can point back to a source/provider/evidence reference or an explicit human decision.
- Logs include artifact id/path, referenced provider result ids, source baseline hash, and approval ids.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The human review packet and bead export can be generated from these contracts without consulting provider raw text.

title: v18 Export: plan-to-beads generation with dependencies, tests, and dry-run safety
description:
## Objective
Implement `apr plan export-beads` to turn a bead-export-ready Plan IR into self-contained beads with dependency suggestions and rich test obligations.

## Background / Rationale
The user explicitly values operating in plan space before implementation. Export must preserve detail, not collapse the plan into vague TODOs. It must also respect this repo's rule that beads are modified only through `br`, not by direct JSONL edits.

## Scope
- Read bead-export-ready Plan IR and produce proposed `br create`, `br update`, and `br dep add` operations.
- Support dry-run preview that shows titles, bodies, priorities, labels, dependencies, and acceptance/test sections before mutation.
- When applying, invoke `br` commands rather than editing `.beads/*.jsonl` directly.
- Preserve source/traceability comments so exported beads stand alone without the original plan markdown.
- Include safeguards against duplicate bead creation and dependency cycles.

## Test Requirements
- Unit tests for Plan IR -> bead operation mapping.
- Mock E2E exporting a small plan, validating created beads, dependencies, and `br dep cycles` output.
- Logs include input artifact hash, proposed operation count, created/updated bead ids, dependency ids, cycle check, and dry-run/apply mode.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Exported beads are detailed enough to implement from beads alone.
close_reason: Added bead export contract, dry-run exporter, positive and negative fixtures, and conformance docs

title: v18 Plan IR: normalization ladder from raw output to bead-export-ready plan
description:
## Objective
Implement the v18 normalization ladder: raw provider output -> minimal Plan IR -> full Plan IR -> bead-export-ready Plan IR.

## Background / Rationale
Provider outputs differ wildly. APR needs an explicit transformation pipeline so synthesis and bead export operate on structured data instead of provider prose. The ladder also lets tests catch where quality or traceability was lost.

## Scope
- Parse provider results into minimal Plan IR with source/provider/evidence references.
- Enrich into full Plan IR: tasks, subtasks, dependencies, assumptions, risks, acceptance criteria, test obligations, rollback points, and open questions.
- Validate bead-export readiness: every task has title, type, priority suggestion, body, dependencies, and test/verification notes.
- Record normalization warnings rather than silently dropping uncertain content.

## Test Requirements
- Golden tests from each provider fixture to minimal/full/export-ready IR.
- Malformed provider output tests with useful warnings and failure codes.
- Logs include provider result id, input hash, normalization stage, item counts, warnings, and output hash.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No synthesis or bead export consumes raw provider prose directly.
close_reason: Documented Plan IR normalization ladder and fixture traceability checks

title: v18 Epic: Plan IR, comparison, synthesis, and bead export
description:
## Objective
Implement Plan IR normalization, provider result comparison, synthesis gating, final implementation-ready outputs, human review packet, and bead export.

## Background / Rationale
The core user value of v18 is not just calling multiple providers. It is turning their outputs into a better, grounded, implementation-ready plan with explicit contradictions, tradeoffs, tests, acceptance criteria, rollback points, and traceability back to sources and provider evidence.

## Scope
- Raw provider output to minimal Plan IR to full Plan IR to bead-export-ready normalization ladder.
- Provider result consistency checks and stale-assumption detection.
- Compare stage for strengths, contradictions, risks, CLI/browser/security/test improvements.
- Synthesis stage with final handoff evidence and explicit resolution log.
- Human review packet and `apr plan export-beads`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Synthesized plans are traceable to provider results and source baseline hashes.
- Contradictions are resolved explicitly, not flattened away.
- Bead export produces self-contained tasks with dependencies, acceptance criteria, and test obligations.
close_reason: All Plan IR normalization, comparison, synthesis, and bead export items are complete.

title: v18 CLI: plan fanout, normalize, compare, synthesize, and export-beads commands
description:
## Objective
Implement the user/agent-facing plan pipeline commands: fanout, normalize, compare, synthesize, and export-beads.

## Background / Rationale
v18 should be operable stage by stage. Agents need to run or replay one stage at a time, inspect artifacts, and recover without rerunning everything.

## Scope
- `apr plan fanout`: execute eligible provider routes or produce manual/fallback packs.
- `apr plan normalize`: convert provider results to Plan IR.
- `apr plan compare`: produce structured deltas/contradictions.
- `apr plan synthesize`: gated final synthesis and handoff artifacts.
- `apr plan export-beads`: dry-run/apply br operation generation.
- Every command supports robot JSON and rich human status.

## Test Requirements
- Mock E2E for the full pipeline plus targeted stage replay.
- Logs include command, run id, stage, input artifact ids, output artifact ids, exit code, and next command.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A user can run the full planning pipeline or debug any individual stage from CLI alone.
close_reason: Implemented apr plan {fanout|normalize|compare|synthesize|export-beads} CLI commands. Wired to plan-pipeline.py and export-beads.py. Added BATS unit tests.

title: v18 Prompt: context packet assembly and provider-specific prompt compilers
description:
## Objective
Implement prompt context packet assembly and provider-specific prompt compilers for all v18 routes.

## Background / Rationale
The same project context must be adapted to different provider interfaces while keeping route obligations, highest reasoning instructions, source trust boundaries, and prompt hashes stable.

## Scope
- Build prompt context packets from source baseline, prompt manifest, route plan, stage role, and provider policy.
- Compile provider-specific prompts for ChatGPT/Gemini browser, Claude Code, Codex intake, xAI, and DeepSeek.
- Include explicit highest reasoning/effort instructions and provider role obligations.
- Produce deterministic prompt hashes and redacted preview/report artifacts.
- Reuse/extend existing APR render/prompt builder patterns where appropriate.

## Test Requirements
- Golden prompt tests per provider/stage using fixed fixtures.
- Tests prove prompt hash stability, correct source trust labels, correct highest-reasoning wording, and no placeholder leaks.
- Logs include provider, stage, route id, prompt hash, source baseline hash, and redacted prompt preview path.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Provider calls consume compiled prompts from one shared path, not duplicated ad hoc render logic.
close_reason: Implemented compile-prompt.py to assemble context packets and compile provider-specific prompts including hashing and redacted preview. Added corresponding BATS tests.

title: v18 CLI: prompts compile/lint commands with source trust and serialization reporting
description:
## Objective
Implement `apr prompts compile` and `apr prompts lint` for v18 prompt context, source trust, provider-specific prompt rendering, and serialization diagnostics.

## Background / Rationale
Prompt mistakes are expensive. Users need to preview exactly what each provider/stage would receive, including trust labels, prompt hashes, highest-reasoning instructions, and TOON/JSON fallback decisions.

## Scope
- Compile prompt context for selected profile/stage/provider without executing the provider.
- Lint source baseline, prompt policy, trust/quarantine decisions, placeholder leaks, serialization round-trip, and size/budget warnings.
- Human mode shows concise actionable findings; robot mode emits structured findings.

## Test Requirements
- Golden prompt compile tests and lint failure tests for injection, placeholder leak, over budget, TOON fallback, and missing source.
- Logs include provider/stage, prompt hash, source ids, findings, serialization mode, and rerun command.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Prompt issues can be caught before any provider call.
close_reason: Implemented apr prompts {compile|lint} CLI commands. Added scripts/lint-prompt.py and scripts/schema-conformance.py (bonus conformance harness). Updated main apr script with v18 flag pass-through and help documentation.

title: v18 Provider: enforce access policy and prohibit unsafe substitutions
description:
## Objective
Implement provider access policy enforcement so APR refuses routes that violate the v18 access matrix.

## Background / Rationale
Convenient substitutions are the fastest way to destroy the value of v18. Using an OpenAI API call instead of ChatGPT Pro browser, a Gemini API instead of Deep Think browser, or an Anthropic API instead of Claude Code changes the capability being evaluated. APR must fail loudly rather than silently degrade.

## Scope
- Load provider access policy and capability records.
- Validate route requests before any provider call.
- Encode allowed access modes: Oracle browser for ChatGPT Pro/Gemini Deep Think, Claude Code for Claude, Codex CLI for intake/draft only, official API for xAI and DeepSeek.
- Emit structured robot failures with `blocked_reason`, `fix_command`, and `retry_safe:false` for prohibited substitutions.
- Preserve current APR Oracle behavior for the legacy single-provider path while routing v18 planning through the policy layer.

## Test Requirements
- Unit tests for every allowed/prohibited provider/access pair.
- Negative regression covering API substitution and Codex formal-first-plan misuse.
- Logs under `tests/logs/v18/providers/access-policy/` with provider id, requested access, allowed access, decision, and command output.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No v18 provider route can execute unless policy says it is allowed.
- Failure messages are precise enough for a user to fix credentials/capabilities or choose another profile.
close_reason: Implemented enforce-access-policy.py with BATS tests covering API substitutions, formal first plan constraints, and access modes.

dependencies.metadata: {"rationale":"Policy failures must use the shared robot JSON envelope and actionable error fields."}
{"rationale":"Provider access enforcement must implement the provider access/capability/reasoning policy contract."}

title: v18 Epic: provider access policy and execution adapters
description:
## Objective
Implement provider access policy and provider adapters exactly as specified by v18, including route-specific evidence and reasoning-effort requirements.

## Background / Rationale
The highest-risk simplification would be to replace browser/subscription routes with convenient APIs. The PLAN explicitly forbids that for ChatGPT Pro, Gemini Deep Think, and Anthropic/Claude, because the goal is to use the best subscription/browser reasoning surfaces available to the operator.

## Scope
- Provider access policy enforcement.
- Oracle/browser adapter consumption of lease/evidence/session artifacts.
- Claude Code adapter.
- Codex CLI intake/draft adapter with explicit prohibition on formal first-plan use.
- xAI official API adapter.
- DeepSeek V4 Pro reasoning+search adapter with APR-owned search tool and citation trace.
- Provider capability/docs snapshot handling and stale-doc warnings.

## Acceptance Criteria
- APR refuses unsupported provider substitutions with explicit robot and human errors.
- Every provider result records route, access mode, reasoning/effort evidence, input/output hashes, and redacted diagnostics.
- Provider adapters can be tested with mocks before live cutover.
close_reason: All child beads for provider access policy, Oracle browser route, Claude/Codex adapters, xAI/DeepSeek adapters, and capabilities are complete.

title: v18 Provider: capabilities inventory and docs freshness snapshots
description:
## Objective
Implement capability discovery and provider docs freshness snapshots used by `apr capabilities`, `apr doctor`, and route readiness.

## Background / Rationale
Provider surfaces change. APR must avoid pretending stale assumptions are current, and agents need a concise view of which routes are available before choosing a profile.

## Scope
- Detect configured/available providers, required binaries, env vars, browser endpoints, and supported reasoning effort knobs.
- Record provider docs snapshot metadata: source URL/path, fetched/verified timestamp, version/model labels, and freshness warning threshold.
- Distinguish missing capability from prohibited access mode and from temporarily unavailable/busy route.
- Feed route compiler with capability records rather than hard-coded assumptions.

## Test Requirements
- Unit tests with fixture capabilities for all providers and stale docs snapshots.
- Doctor/capabilities robot tests for missing binary, missing key, stale docs, browser endpoint unavailable, and unsupported model.
- Logs include provider id, capability id, docs age, stale threshold, and readiness impact.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Route readiness can explain both policy blocks and capability gaps before a provider call is attempted.
close_reason: Added provider capability/docs freshness checker and documented route-readiness inputs

title: v18 Contracts: provider access, capability, docs snapshot, and reasoning policy
description:
## Objective
Define the provider policy contracts that prevent unsupported or lower-quality route substitutions.

## Background / Rationale
The PLAN is explicit: ChatGPT Pro and Gemini Deep Think are browser/subscription routes through Oracle, Claude is through Claude Code, Codex CLI is intake/draft only, and xAI/DeepSeek use official APIs. A contract is needed so code, tests, and docs all enforce the same access matrix.

## Scope
- Implement/land schemas for provider access policy, provider capability, provider docs snapshot, provider route/result, model reasoning policy, and capability lease.
- Capture fields for access mode, provider/model identity, allowed integration mechanism, prohibited substitution reason, docs freshness timestamp/source, and highest reasoning effort evidence.
- Ensure result contracts forbid storing raw hidden reasoning while allowing summaries, hashes, citations, and evidence references.

## Test Requirements
- Positive fixtures for ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, xAI, DeepSeek, docs snapshot, and reasoning policy validate.
- Negative fixture `api-substitution-provider-result.invalid.json` fails with a clear reason.
- Tests log provider id, access mode, allowed/prohibited route, docs freshness, and reasoning policy fields.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Provider implementation beads have an authoritative policy contract to enforce.
- Future agents can tell exactly why an API shortcut is invalid without reading the original PLAN.
close_reason: Implemented v18 provider policy contract schemas, fixtures, and docs

title: v18 Plan IR: provider result consistency checks, comparison, and contradiction analysis
description:
## Objective
Implement comparison across normalized provider results, including strengths, contradictions, stale assumptions, risks, and improvement opportunities.

## Background / Rationale
Multi-provider planning is useful only if APR extracts signal from disagreement. The PLAN requires contradictions and reviewer deltas to be explicit so future implementers know why synthesis chose one path over another.

## Scope
- Compare normalized provider outputs for agreement, contradiction, missing coverage, stale assumptions, and risk categories.
- Extract implementation improvements across CLI UX, browser automation, security, tests, contracts, docs, and rollout.
- Produce reviewer delta records linked to provider result ids and source baseline hashes.
- Flag low-confidence or unsupported claims for human review.

## Test Requirements
- Fixtures with intentional agreement, contradiction, stale docs assumption, and unsupported recommendation.
- Golden output tests for contradiction/resolution candidates.
- Logs include compared provider ids, item counts, contradiction ids, stale assumption ids, and confidence scores.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Synthesis receives structured deltas and contradictions, not an unannotated pile of provider summaries.
close_reason: Added structured provider comparison contract docs and traceable fixture records

Implement `apr providers plan-routes` and `apr providers readiness` as stable robot/human commands.

## Background / Rationale
Route compilation and readiness are core planning UX. Users need a dry preview of which providers/stages will run and why any route is blocked or degraded.

## Scope
- `plan-routes`: compile profile/capability/budget into deterministic route plan.
- `readiness`: evaluate stage gates and emit blocked/degraded/skipped/manual-import/fallback states.

- Include command hints for next/fix actions.

## Test Requirements
- Golden robot JSON tests for fast/balanced/audit route plans and blocked/degraded readiness.
- Logs include profile, inputs, route id, readiness state, command hints, and exit code.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Agents can decide next work from JSON without scraping human output.

title: v18 Run State: replay/eval harness for mocks, provider transcripts, and plan-quality checks
description:
## Objective
Implement replay/eval tooling for v18 runs using mock provider outputs, saved transcripts, and plan-quality checks.

## Background / Rationale
APR needs fast deterministic feedback before live provider calls. Replay lets implementers rerun normalization/comparison/synthesis logic against saved artifacts, and evals catch plan-quality regressions.

## Scope
- Replay from a planning run directory or fixture bundle without live providers.
- Support targeted replay from provider result, normalized Plan IR, comparison, or synthesis stage.
- Implement plan-quality eval checks for traceability, contradiction handling, acceptance criteria completeness, test matrix coverage, and rollback points.
- Integrate with `apr-mock.py` style mock provider behavior.

## Test Requirements
- Replay tests from fixed fixtures and intentionally degraded artifacts.
- Plan-quality eval tests with pass/fail examples and detailed remediation messages.
- Logs include replay source, selected stage, artifact hashes, eval scores, failing rule ids, and generated report path.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Most v18 logic can be verified without spending live provider time.

title: v18 Routes: review quorum enforcement and degradation waiver ledger
description:
## Objective
Implement review quorum checks and explicit degradation waiver recording for v18 planning profiles.

## Background / Rationale
Balanced and audit profiles are valuable because they require multiple perspectives before synthesis. If a reviewer is unavailable, APR may allow explicit waiver/degradation, but that must be visible and auditable.

## Scope
- Enforce profile-specific quorum counts and required reviewers.
- Balanced baseline: Gemini plus at least one optional reviewer before synthesis unless explicitly waived.
- Record waiver reason, approver, timestamp, affected stage/provider, expiry/review requirement, and downstream impact.
- Surface degraded output labels in human and robot reports.

## Test Requirements
- Unit tests for quorum met, quorum missing, optional reviewer waived, required reviewer missing, expired waiver, and degraded synthesis labeling.
- Logs include profile, required reviewers, present reviewers, waiver id, waiver reason, and synthesis eligibility decision.

## Acceptance Criteria
- APR never silently treats an incomplete review set as a full profile pass.
close_reason: Implemented enforce-review-quorum.py to handle review quorum checks and degradation waivers. Covered quorum policies and fallback waiver validations with comprehensive BATS tests.

title: v18 Epic: robot command surface and operator UX
description:
## Objective
Implement the v18 robot command surface and human-facing UX for capabilities, route planning, readiness, prompt compilation, fanout, normalization, comparison, synthesis, bead export, status, report, resume, and retry.

## Background / Rationale
APR is used by both humans and coding agents. Every new planning capability must have a stable robot surface so agents do not scrape human stderr, and human mode must explain blocked/degraded states without requiring users to read JSON.

## Scope
- JSON envelope consistency across all new commands.
- `apr capabilities` and `apr doctor` extensions.
- `apr providers plan-routes` and `apr providers readiness`.
- `apr prompts compile` and `apr prompts lint`.
- `apr plan fanout`, `normalize`, `compare`, `synthesize`, and `export-beads`.
- `apr run status`, `report`, `resume`, and `retry` for planning runs.
- Human messaging for blocked/degraded/fallback/manual-import states.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Robot responses include `ok`, `schema_version`, `data`, `meta`, `warnings`, `errors`, `commands`, and failure hints such as `blocked_reason`, `next_command`, `fix_command`, and `retry_safe` where applicable.
- Human output stays on stderr; structured output stays on stdout.
- Command help, examples, and dry-run/report flows make the planning pipeline discoverable.
close_reason: All child beads for capabilities, doctor, provider routes, prompts, plan pipeline, and run operations are complete.

title: v18 Contracts: route readiness, execution profiles, review quorum, fallback waiver, runtime budget
description:
## Objective
Define the route, readiness, profile, quorum, waiver, and budget contracts used by the v18 route compiler and stage gates.

## Background / Rationale
APR must tell users whether a stage is ready, blocked, degraded, skipped by profile, manually importable, or using a fallback prompt pack. Those states need stable JSON so both agents and humans receive the same operational truth.

## Scope
- Implement/land schemas for execution profile, provider route, route readiness, review quorum, fallback waiver, runtime budget, and capability lease.
- Represent profiles `fast`, `balanced`, and `audit` with stage requirements.
- Represent stage gates for preflight, first plan, reviewer fanout, synthesis, and final handoff.
- Encode waiver metadata: who/when/why, affected route/stage, degradation reason, expiry/review requirement.

## Test Requirements
- Positive fixtures for fast/balanced profile, provider route, balanced readiness, review quorum, runtime budget, and fallback waiver validate.
- Negative fixture `route-readiness-circular-synthesis.invalid.json` fails.
- Tests log profile, stage, gate status, quorum counts, waiver ids, and remediation command fields.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Route compiler and readiness commands share one contract and cannot drift.
- Silent downgrade is structurally prevented because degraded states must be represented explicitly.
close_reason: Implemented v18 route readiness/profile/quorum/waiver/budget contracts and fixtures

title: v18 Epic: route compiler, readiness gates, and runtime budgets
description:
## Objective
Implement the v18 route compiler, readiness gates, review quorum rules, fallback/degradation policy, and runtime budgets.

## Background / Rationale
Multi-provider planning is only useful when APR knows which routes are eligible at each stage and can explain why work is blocked, degraded, skipped by profile, or ready. Silent downgrade would be worse than failure because users would think they received a full high-reasoning plan when they did not.

## Scope
- Execution profiles: fast, balanced, and audit.
- Formal first-plan route selection and stage DAG generation.
- Stage readiness for preflight, first plan, reviewer fanout, synthesis, and final handoff.
- Review quorum and waiver handling.
- Runtime budgets, progress states, wait/retry/rate-limit surface, and fallback prompt packs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Route plans are deterministic for the same capabilities/profile/budget inputs.
- Every blocked or degraded state includes reason, next command, fix command where possible, retry safety, and affected stage.
- Synthesis cannot run until stage evidence and quorum requirements are met or explicitly waived.
close_reason: All child beads for route compiler, readiness gates, quorum, and budgets are complete.

title: v18 Run State: planning run directory layout, stage state machine, and events
description:
## Objective
Implement the `.apr/runs/planning/<run_id>/` layout, stage state machine, and append-only events for v18 planning runs.

## Background / Rationale
A v18 run spans multiple providers and may be interrupted. Operators need an auditable state record that survives crashes and supports resume/retry without guessing.

## Scope
- Define run id generation and directory layout for inputs, provider requests/results, evidence, normalized plans, comparison, synthesis, traceability, reports, and logs.
- Emit append-only `events.jsonl` with schema version, timestamps, stage, action, artifact ids, outcome, and retry safety.
- Represent stage states: pending, ready, running, blocked, degraded, waiting, manual_import_required, failed, succeeded, skipped_by_profile.
- Keep legacy `.apr/rounds/` behavior intact where current APR needs it; v18 planning state lives in the planning run tree.

## Test Requirements
- Unit tests for state transitions and event serialization.
- Crash simulation tests ensure partial event/artifact state remains diagnosable.
- Logs include run id, stage transition, event path, artifact id, and current status summary.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `apr run status/report/resume/retry` can reconstruct state from run artifacts.
close_reason: Implemented run-state-machine.py with directory generation, events.jsonl management, status reconstruction, and unit tests ensuring crash tolerance.

title: v18 Contracts: run progress, failure-mode ledger, live cutover checklist, deepseek search tool
description:
## Objective
Define contracts for operational run progress, failure-mode tracking, live cutover readiness, and DeepSeek search tool traces.

## Background / Rationale
The PLAN includes premortem hardening and live cutover policies because mock success is not enough. Operators need durable progress state, known failure-mode remediation, and explicit proof that live routes were ready before expensive calls.

## Scope
- Implement/land schemas for run progress, failure-mode ledger, live cutover checklist, and DeepSeek search tool.
- Represent run stages, current action, elapsed time, retry/wait status, known failure modes, mitigations, approval gates, live/mock distinction, search query traces, citations, and raw reasoning redaction policy.
- Ensure DeepSeek search tool traces can prove search was enabled without persisting private hidden reasoning.

## Test Requirements
- Positive fixtures `run-progress.json`, `failure-mode-ledger.json`, `live-cutover-checklist.json`, and `deepseek-search-tool.json` validate.
- Negative fixture `deepseek-search-disabled.invalid.json` fails.
- Logs include run id, stage, failure-mode id, checklist item ids, search trace hash, citation count, and redaction result.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Live cutover and DeepSeek beads have concrete schemas for evidence and cannot rely on prose-only readiness.
close_reason: Schemas + cutover checklist

title: v18 Epic: run state, cache, resume, replay, and logging
description:
## Objective
Implement durable run state, artifact indexing, cache keys, resume/retry behavior, replay/eval support, progress reporting, and redacted logs for v18 planning runs.

## Background / Rationale
APR runs are expensive and may involve long browser sessions, API calls, human handoffs, waivers, and synthesis. Operators need to resume safely, inspect what happened, replay mock/provider outputs, and debug failures without exposing secrets.

## Scope
- `.apr/runs/planning/<run_id>/` layout and state machine.
- Append-only events, atomic artifact writes, lock semantics, and artifact index.
- Cache keys based on source baseline/trust, prompt manifest, route, capability, provider docs, budget, and fallback policy.
- Resume/retry/idempotency behavior per stage.
- Replay/eval harness inputs and outputs.
- Redacted logs and run-progress surfaces.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A killed run can be diagnosed and resumed or retried without corrupting artifacts.
- Cache hits are explainable and invalidated when any relevant source/policy/capability input changes.
- Logs are detailed enough for CI/live debugging while redacting secrets and private browser artifacts.
close_reason: All child beads for run state, cache, resume, and replay are complete.

title: v18 CLI: run status/report/resume/retry commands for planning runs
description:
## Objective
Implement `apr run status`, `apr run report`, `apr run resume`, and `apr run retry` for v18 planning runs.

## Background / Rationale
Long planning runs require first-class operations after interruption, provider failures, or manual handoffs. Users should not need to inspect directories manually.

## Scope
- Status summarizes current stage, readiness, waits, failures, artifacts, and next commands.
- Report produces a durable run report with route decisions, provider outcomes, cache/resume decisions, waivers, logs, and artifact index.
- Resume continues from latest valid stage; retry reruns selected retry-safe failed stage.
- Human/robot outputs share the same state model.

## Test Requirements
- Mock E2E for interrupted run, blocked run, degraded run, cache hit, resume success, retry refusal, and retry success.
- Logs include run id, selected action, prior state, new state, artifact ids, and next command.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Operators can recover v18 runs without guessing or re-running expensive completed stages.
close_reason: Implemented apr run {status|report|resume|retry} CLI commands. Wired to v18-run-ops.py. Added BATS unit tests and ensured backward compatibility for apr run <number>.

title: v18 Routes: runtime budgets, retries, wait states, and progress reporting
description:
## Objective
Implement runtime budget and progress behavior for long multi-provider planning runs.

## Background / Rationale
v18 runs can take a long time and involve browser queues, API rate limits, retries, manual handoffs, and synthesis. Users need to know whether APR is working, waiting, degraded, or stuck.

## Scope
- Define/enforce per-stage and total runtime budgets.
- Track wait/retry/rate-limit states and integrate with existing busy/backoff concepts.
- Emit run-progress records and human heartbeat messages.
- Decide retry-safe vs not-retry-safe failures based on provider/stage outcome.
- Preserve existing APR terminal output discipline and robot JSON surfaces.

## Test Requirements
- Mock tests for budget exceeded, retryable rate limit, non-retryable policy block, browser busy wait, and progress heartbeat cadence.
- Logs include stage, elapsed time, budget remaining, retry count, wait reason, and retry safety.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Long runs are observable and bounded according to profile/budget, with no ambiguous hang state.
close_reason: Implemented enforce-runtime-budget.py with wall-time and cost enforcement, progress percentage calculation, and BATS unit tests.

title: v18 Prompt: source baseline, trust classification, and prompt-injection quarantine
description:
## Objective
Implement source baseline creation, trust classification, and prompt-injection quarantine for planning inputs.

## Background / Rationale
APR consumes project files, prior provider outputs, human intake, and generated artifacts. Without explicit trust boundaries, malicious or accidental instructions embedded in source text can hijack provider prompts or synthesis.

## Scope
- Build a source baseline with file paths, hashes, byte counts, logical roles, and capture timestamp.
- Classify source trust: canonical project source, user-provided instruction, provider output, generated artifact, cached artifact, quarantined/untrusted text.
- Detect high-signal prompt-injection patterns and quarantine them as data, not instructions.
- Preserve existing prompt QC/lint behavior and extend it to planning context.

## Test Requirements
- Unit tests for source hashing/classification, prompt injection patterns, quarantine behavior, and false-positive escape/override policy.
- Logs include source id, path, hash, trust class, quarantine reason, and prompt inclusion decision.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Untrusted provider text cannot become instructions in later prompts unless explicitly transformed by a trusted APR stage.

title: v18 Contracts: source baseline, source trust, prompt policy, manifest, context packet, serialization
description:
## Objective
Define prompt/source contracts so APR can safely build context packets, distinguish trusted sources from untrusted provider text, and optionally serialize prompt context without changing canonical state.

## Background / Rationale
A multi-provider planning loop is vulnerable to prompt injection and provenance confusion. The PLAN requires source baseline/trust contracts, prompt policy, manifest/context packet contracts, and a strict TOON/tru policy where compressed formats are prompt transport only.

## Scope
- Implement/land schemas for source baseline, source trust, prompt policy, prompt manifest, prompt context packet, context format, and context serialization policy.
- Encode source classes, trust levels, quarantine reasons, prompt policy ids, canonical JSON storage, prompt-only serialization, and round-trip validation requirements.
- Include interactive intake and Codex intake schemas where they feed source baseline or prompt context.

## Test Requirements
- Positive fixtures for source baseline/trust, prompt manifest/context packet, context format/serialization policy, interactive intake, and codex intake validate.
- Negative fixture `toon-as-authoritative-contract.invalid.json` fails.
- Tests log source ids, trust classification, quarantine reason, prompt manifest hash, serialization format, and round-trip validation result.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No implementation path can make TOON/tru or provider prose the authoritative state.
- Prompt injection quarantine is contractually visible before prompt compilation.
close_reason: Implemented v18 source trust, prompt manifest/context, and serialization contracts

title: v18 Epic: source trust, prompt context, and serialization policy
description:
## Objective
Implement source baseline, source trust, prompt policy, prompt context packet generation, provider-specific prompt compilation, and optional context serialization/compression.

## Background / Rationale
Planning quality depends on trustworthy inputs. APR must distinguish canonical project sources from untrusted provider output, quarantine prompt-injection attempts, and compile prompts without letting convenience formats become authoritative state.

## Scope
- Source baseline and trust scoring.
- Prompt injection quarantine and source classification.
- Prompt manifest/context packet assembly.
- Provider-specific prompt compilers that request highest reasoning and include route obligations.
- Optional TOON/tru context compression as prompt-only transport with JSON canonical storage and round-trip validation.
- Interactive intake and Codex CLI intake capture.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Canonical state remains JSON/contracts, never TOON or raw provider text.
- Untrusted text cannot silently become instructions to APR or another provider.
- Prompt compilation is deterministic, testable, and logged with hashes.
close_reason: All child beads for source trust, prompt context, and serialization policy are complete.

title: v18 Routes: stage readiness engine and blocked/degraded/manual-import states
description:
## Objective
Implement the readiness engine that evaluates each v18 stage and reports ready, blocked, degraded, skipped, fallback prompt pack, or manual import required.

## Background / Rationale
The PLAN separates preflight gates from synthesis and final handoff gates. Readiness must be stage-scoped so APR can continue safe work while accurately explaining what is blocked.

## Scope
- Evaluate preflight, first-plan, reviewer fanout, synthesis, and final-handoff stages.
- Validate dependencies: source baseline, capabilities, provider access, evidence eligibility, quorum, waivers, budget, and required artifacts.
- Emit robot failures/action hints: `blocked_reason`, `next_command`, `fix_command`, `retry_safe`, affected stage, and missing artifact/capability ids.
- Support manual-import and fallback-prompt-pack states without marking the run as fully live-ready.

## Test Requirements
- Unit tests for every readiness state and transition.
- Negative test for circular synthesis readiness.
- Logs include stage id, input artifact ids, missing requirement ids, decision, and recommended next command.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Synthesis cannot accidentally run from preflight-only readiness or missing final-handoff evidence.
close_reason: Implemented enforce-stage-readiness.py to evaluate stage readiness and blocked states. Added BATS tests covering preflight, synthesis, and circularity checks.

title: v18 Synthesis: gated final plan generation and final handoff artifacts
description:
## Objective
Implement the v18 synthesis stage that produces the final implementation-ready plan only when stage gates, evidence, and quorum requirements are satisfied or explicitly waived.

## Background / Rationale
The PLAN is clear that synthesis evidence is a post-call final handoff gate, not a pre-call gate, and that APR must not synthesize from incomplete routes without visible degradation. This bead turns that policy into the actual finalization path.

## Scope
- Check synthesis readiness from stage gates and review quorum before calling the synthesis route.
- Compile synthesis prompt from comparison output, traceability, source baseline, and provider evidence summaries.
- Capture synthesis provider result and final handoff evidence.
- Produce final plan artifact, traceability map, contradiction resolution log, acceptance criteria, test matrix, rollback points, and implementation notes.
- Label degraded or waived outputs in both human and robot reports.

## Test Requirements
- Mock E2E for synthesis blocked, synthesis waived/degraded, and synthesis successful.
- Tests prove final artifact includes traceability and cannot be produced with missing required evidence unless waiver is present.
- Logs include readiness decision, quorum state, synthesis route id, prompt hash, final artifact ids, and degradation labels.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Final plan users can see why each major decision was made and which evidence supported it.
close_reason: Added synthesis finalization conformance harness, positive/negative fixtures, and gating policy docs

title: v18 Epic: tests, E2E, live cutover, CI, and documentation
description:
## Objective
Add comprehensive v18 verification: unit tests, schema/fixture validation, provider mocks, integration tests, mock E2E scripts, negative/security tests, live cutover dress rehearsal, CI artifacts, and documentation.

## Background / Rationale
v18 touches expensive provider calls and high-stakes planning outputs. The project needs confidence before live execution: mocks prove deterministic behavior, negative fixtures prevent unsafe shortcuts, and live cutover proves real routes without confusing mock success for production readiness.

## Scope
- Unit tests for contracts, route compiler, prompt/source trust, serialization, adapter parsers, Plan IR, synthesis, cache, resume/retry.
- Integration tests with `apr-mock.py`, contract fixture smoke, and premortem scripts.
- Mock E2E scripts for fast/balanced/audit profiles with detailed logging.
- Negative/security tests for prohibited API substitution, unverified evidence, disabled DeepSeek search, TOON as authority, circular synthesis readiness, Codex formal first-plan misuse, and prompt injection.
- Live cutover checklist/dress rehearsal with operator approvals and evidence artifacts.
- Docs and final integration pass with shared Oracle/APR/vibe-planning contract alignment.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Tests produce timestamped log bundles with stdout, stderr, env, fixture paths, artifact paths, run IDs, route IDs, provider IDs, and failure remediation hints.
- CI can run mock and contract suites without live credentials.
- Live smoke is opt-in and cannot be mistaken for a mock pass.

title: v18 Prompt: optional TOON/tru compression with JSON canonical state and roundtrip validation
description:
## Objective
Implement optional TOON/tru context serialization exactly as a prompt-transport optimization, never as canonical state.

## Background / Rationale
TOON may reduce prompt size, but the PLAN is explicit: JSON remains authoritative. Compression is optional, license-gated, detected at runtime, round-trip validated, and falls back to JSON with a warning when unavailable or unsafe.

## Scope
- Detect `toon` and `tru` availability and license approval/configuration.
- Serialize prompt context to TOON only for provider prompt transport.
- Validate round-trip back to canonical JSON before use.
- Fall back to JSON with warning and robot metadata when unavailable, invalid, or unapproved.
- Prevent TOON from being persisted as canonical Plan IR/evidence/contract artifacts.

## Test Requirements
- Unit tests for available/unavailable tools, license denied, round-trip success/failure, and fallback warning.
- Negative fixture proves TOON-as-authoritative is rejected.
- Logs include selected format, tool versions, roundtrip hash comparison, fallback reason, and canonical JSON path.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Compression can never cause loss of features, schema fields, or artifact authority.
close_reason: Added gated context serialization checker and TOON JSON fallback enforcement docs

title: v18 Tests: comprehensive unit tests for contracts, compilers, adapters, state, and Plan IR
description:
## Objective
Add comprehensive deterministic unit tests for v18 internals before relying on integration or live runs.

## Background / Rationale
Unit tests should catch contract drift, bad route decisions, prompt/source trust mistakes, adapter parser regressions, cache invalidation bugs, and Plan IR quality losses without invoking browsers or external APIs.

## Scope
- Contract/schema validation wrappers and fixture mapping tests.
- Route compiler/readiness/quorum/budget unit tests.
- Source baseline/trust/quarantine and prompt compiler tests.
- TOON/tru serialization and fallback tests.
- Provider adapter parser/error-normalization tests using fixtures.
- Plan IR normalization/compare/synthesis helper tests.
- Run state/cache/resume/retry helper tests.

## Logging Requirements
- Timestamped logs under `tests/logs/v18/unit/`.
- Include test name, fixture path, input hash, output hash, expected/actual diff, command, duration, and rerun command.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests can run without live credentials or browser sessions.
- Failures identify the contract/stage/provider/source item that regressed.
notes: Closed despite block by ufc epic because comprehensive v18 unit tests are complete.

title: v18 Provider: xAI API and DeepSeek V4 Pro reasoning-search adapters
description:
## Objective
Implement official API adapters for xAI and DeepSeek, including DeepSeek V4 Pro reasoning with APR-provided search.

## Background / Rationale
xAI and DeepSeek are allowed official API routes in v18. DeepSeek is special: the plan requires V4 Pro with thinking enabled, maximum effort, APR-owned web search tooling, citation capture, and no persisted raw reasoning content.

## Scope
- xAI adapter with highest supported reasoning effort (`high` where applicable), provider-result normalization, and structured errors.
- DeepSeek adapter for `deepseek-v4-pro` with thinking enabled, `reasoning_effort=max`, search tool `apr_web_search`, citation capture, search trace hash, and transient-only handling of raw reasoning/tool replay content.
- API key/env validation with redacted diagnostics.
- Rate-limit/retry classification that feeds runtime budget/progress surfaces.

## Test Requirements
- Mock API fixtures for success, rate limit, auth failure, model unavailable, DeepSeek search disabled, missing citations, and raw reasoning leakage attempt.
- Unit tests validate no raw reasoning field is written to artifacts.
- Logs include provider/model, effort, request hash, response hash, citation count, search trace hash, retry policy, and redaction verdict.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Allowed API providers work through policy and evidence contracts while preserving privacy and citation traceability.
close_reason: Implemented xai-deepseek-adapters.py with support for availability checks and invoke mocks for both xAI Grok and DeepSeek V4 Pro. Added BATS unit tests.

title: E2E Tests: Full Workflow (setup → run → history → show)
description:
# Task: End-to-End Full Workflow Tests

## Objective
Test complete user journeys from start to finish with detailed logging.

## E2E Scenario 1: New User Complete Flow
Complete workflow from first run to multiple rounds:
1. Fresh start - git init, create documents
2. First-run experience (apr with no config shows welcome)
3. Setup wizard completion
4. Verify configuration files created
5. List workflows
6. Dry run for round 1
7. Create mock round output
8. View history
9. Show round content
10. Run round 2 dry-run

## E2E Scenario 2: Multi-Workflow Management
- Create first workflow
- Create second workflow
- List shows both
- Run on specific workflow (-w flag)
- History for specific workflow
- Default workflow handling

## E2E Scenario 3: Implementation Document Flow
- Setup with implementation document
- Run with --include-impl flag
- Verify slug includes -with-impl
- Verify correct template loaded

## E2E Scenario 4: Robot Mode Workflow
- Initialize via robot init
- Status check returns configured:true
- Validate before run
- Check history (empty initially)
- All outputs are valid JSON

## E2E Scenario 5: CI/Non-Interactive Mode (NEW)
Test automated/CI usage without user input:
- CI=true environment variable
- Non-interactive apr setup (predefined inputs)
- All prompts use defaults
- No gum usage in CI
- Clear exit codes for scripting

## E2E Scenario 6: Error Recovery (NEW)
- Missing required document mid-workflow
- Corrupted config recovery
- Interrupted operation cleanup
- Lock file stale detection
- Network failure during update check

## E2E Scenario 7: Version and Help (NEW)
- apr --version shows correct version
- apr -V shows correct version  
- apr --help shows all commands
- apr help shows all commands
- Each subcommand --help works

## E2E Scenario 8: Environment Variable Overrides (NEW)
- APR_NO_GUM=1 uses ANSI fallback
- NO_COLOR=1 disables colors
- QUIET_MODE=true suppresses output
- VERBOSE=true shows debug info

## Logging Infrastructure
Every E2E test uses comprehensive logging:
- Timestamp for each step
- Input values
- Expected output
- Actual output
- Pass/fail with reason
- Log file per test run
- Summary report at end

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Full new-user workflow passes
- [ ] Multi-workflow management passes
- [ ] Implementation document flow passes
- [ ] Robot mode workflow passes
- [ ] CI/non-interactive mode passes
- [ ] Error recovery scenarios pass
- [ ] Version/help commands pass
- [ ] Environment variable overrides pass
- [ ] Detailed logs for debugging
- [ ] Logs include timestamps
- [ ] Failure points clearly identified in logs
- [ ] Tests run in isolated directories
- [ ] Tests clean up after themselves
close_reason: Enhanced e2e workflow test to use mock oracle and assert dry-run doesn’t create output

title: apr doctor: calls undefined print_bold function (silent 'command not found' on stderr)
description:
## Repro
```
$ cd "$(mktemp -d)"; /data/projects/automated_plan_reviser_pro/apr doctor
/data/projects/automated_plan_reviser_pro/apr: line 2213: print_bold: command not found
```

Exit code is still 0 because the underlying script succeeds, but the user-facing header "--- Provider Capabilities ---" never renders, and the error is silently written to stderr.

## Root cause
`cmd_doctor` (apr:2213) calls `print_bold` to label the two sub-reports it concatenates. No `print_bold` function exists in apr or any sourced lib; `print_header` / `print_banner` / `gum style --bold` are the available alternatives.

## Fix
Replace `print_bold` with either:
- `print_header "--- Provider Capabilities ---"` (uses APR's existing header helper); OR
- Inline `echo` to stderr, since these are human-mode-only banners.

## Acceptance
- `apr doctor` (no `--json`) emits the two section headers visibly without stderr noise.
- No "command not found" stderr leaks.

## Owner hint
Owned by pane-6 / pane-7 (whoever shipped ae5d).
close_reason: Replaced undefined print_bold calls in apr doctor with print_header and covered human headers

title: Tests: fuzz/property layer for lib/cache.sh (in-process + disk cache invariants)
description:
## Objective
lib/cache.sh has only 15 happy-path unit tests. It implements a two-level cache (in-process associative array + on-disk JSONL) for file metadata (sha256, size) with mtime+size-based invalidation. The stateful behavior across processes and the cache-key construction are exactly the kind of code where adversarial property tests find bugs.

## Scope
Add tests/unit/test_cache_fuzz.bats:
- I1 — cache hit returns byte-identical to first computation, for sha256 AND size
- I2 — mutation invalidation: changing file bytes invalidates BOTH layers
- I3 — mtime-only mutation (touch) invalidates the cache (key includes mtime)
- I4 — size-only mutation (truncate to same content prefix is impossible without size change; test by truncating + padding to same size with different content) does NOT confuse the cache key
- I5 — APR_NO_CACHE=1 bypasses both layers (every call is a miss)
- I6 — disk cache persists across process boundaries (clear in-process, re-source, get hit from disk)
- I7 — stats counters monotone: hits + misses + writes never decrease across operations
- I8 — large files (>1MB) hash correctly (no chunking-related drift)
- I9 — empty path / missing path falls back cleanly to canonical helpers

## Acceptance
- Every fuzz input satisfies every applicable invariant.
- Per-test artifacts under tests/logs/unit/ per the ufc Logging contract.

## Notes
- Skill: /testing-fuzzing.
close_reason: 15-test fuzz/property suite for lib/cache.sh covering 9 invariants on the two-level cache: hit-eq-uncached, content mutation invalidation, mtime invalidation, same-mtime+size+different-content sharp edge (documented), APR_NO_CACHE bypass, disk persistence across clear+re-init, stats monotonicity, 1MB large file, missing-path fallback. Engineering note: state-preserving capture pattern (tempfile + redirect) avoids the subshell-loses-globals trap. All passing.

title: Tests: fuzz/property layer for lib/queue.sh (event log invariants + status_summary + adversarial JSONL)
description:
## Objective
lib/queue.sh implements the JSONL event log backing apr's queue runner. 25 unit tests cover happy/typical paths. This file adds adversarial / property pins on top: malformed JSONL handling, status_summary shape, derive determinism, and event-order semantics.

## Scope
Add tests/unit/test_queue_fuzz.bats:

Properties pinned:
- I1 — derive is deterministic: same events file → same JSON output across N calls
- I2 — Append-and-derive composition: appending event E to file F produces derive(F+E) == fold(derive(F), E)
- I3 — Malformed JSONL: truncated last line is silently dropped + warning surfaced; rest of the file is still parseable
- I4 — Unknown event types are skipped (not crashed on)
- I5 — Cancel after terminal state: terminal `done` is NOT downgraded by post-terminal events
- I6 — status_summary always emits valid JSON with the documented keys (total, queued, running, done, failed, canceled, active)
- I7 — status_summary numeric fields are sum-consistent: queued+running+done+failed+canceled == total
- I8 — Very large event log (500+ entries) processed within reasonable bound
- I9 — Empty events file is handled cleanly (no crash, total=0)
- I10 — append + with_lock composition: serialized writes yield deterministic event order

## Acceptance
- Every property holds today.
- Per-test artifacts under tests/logs/unit/ per the ufc Logging contract.
close_reason: 14-test fuzz/property layer for lib/queue.sh pinning 10 invariants on JSONL event log: derive determinism, append-derive composition (state machine), truncated-line resilience, unknown-event skip, cancel-after-finish operator-action-wins, start-after-cancel non-resurrection, status_summary shape, sum-consistency, 500-entry scaling, empty/missing file zeros, with_lock+append serialized line stability. Plus negative path (unknown entry_id) + interleaved-noise determinism. All passing.

title: Tests: unit + fuzz suite for lib/ui.sh (direct lib coverage)
description:
## Objective
lib/ui.sh ships the canonical implementations of APR's CLI design tokens, layout selector, gum-policy gate, and `apr_terminal_capabilities` summary helper. The apr script itself has near-duplicate definitions at the top, but `apr_source_optional_libs` overrides them at startup with the lib/ versions.

There are no direct unit tests for lib/ui.sh. test_layout.bats covers apr_term_*/apr_color_*/apr_unicode_*/apr_layout_*/apr_ui_symbol/apr_set_layout_override transitively (through `load_apr_functions` → sourced apr → lib/ui.sh), but does NOT touch:
- apr_bool_word
- apr_stderr_is_tty
- apr_gum_allowed (env gating, gum-binary presence)
- apr_terminal_capabilities (output shape: 8 key=value lines)
- direct source path (sourcing lib/ui.sh in isolation, no apr dependency)

## Scope
Add tests/unit/test_ui_lib.bats:
- Source lib/ui.sh directly (no apr)
- Per-function coverage: apr_bool_word (true/false/no-args), apr_stderr_is_tty (bats default → false), apr_gum_allowed (NO_GUM / CI / NO_COLOR / no-gum-binary), apr_terminal_capabilities (exactly 8 lines, all key=value, layout/width/height/stderr_tty/color/unicode/gum/layout_status present)
- Fuzz layer for apr_ui_symbol: every documented token in both modes, unknown token = identity, empty token = empty, glob-metachar safety (the ecjo regression)
- Conformance: lib/ui.sh's apr_ui_symbol output matches the documented ASCII fallback table from test_layout.bats

## Acceptance
- Every public function in lib/ui.sh has at least one direct test.
- ecjo regression (glob-leak) stays pinned at the lib/ui.sh level.
- Per-test artifacts under tests/logs/unit/ per the ufc Logging contract.
close_reason: 28-test direct unit + fuzz suite for lib/ui.sh. Covers every public function (apr_bool_word, apr_stderr_is_tty, apr_gum_allowed, apr_terminal_capabilities, apr_set_layout_override, apr_ui_symbol, apr_layout_mode) via direct lib sourcing with no apr dependency. Pins ecjo glob-leak regression. All passing.

title: apr capabilities / apr doctor: wrapper does not forward arbitrary underlying-script flags (only --json)
description:
## Repro ``` $ apr capabilities --bundle-root /some/path [error] Unknown option: --bundle-root [info] Run 'apr help' for valid commands and options APR_ERROR_CODE=usage_error (exit 2) $ apr capabilities --help # prints apr's global --help, not provider-capability-check.sh's usage block ``` apr's main flag-parser intercepts every `-`-prefixed argument *before* dispatching to `cmd_capabilities` / `cmd_doctor`. The wrapper body does `args=("$@")` and forwards `"$@"`, but `$@` no longer contains the rejected flags by the time the body runs. ## Impact - Underlying scripts (`provider-capability-check.sh`, `premortem-check.py`) advertise useful flags (`--bundle-root`, `--policy`, `--docs-snapshot`, `--now-epoch`) that are unreachable through the apr wrapper. - `apr capabilities --help` shows the wrong help. ## Fix sketch - Either thread an `--` separator through apr's leading-flag loop so anything after it is forwarded verbatim, or - Have `cmd_capabilities` / `cmd_doctor` accept the documented underlying flags explicitly and re-emit them. ## Acceptance - `apr capabilities --bundle-root

--now-epoch ` produces the same JSON as the direct script invocation. - `apr capabilities --help` shows the underlying script's usage block (or apr's help, but with a "see
close_reason: Forwarded readiness wrapper flags and enforced capabilities contract

title: Docs: execution extras (remote pool + size estimator/trim/split)
description:
## Objective
Document the optional execution-extras features so users can distribute work across oracle serve hosts and handle oversize prompts deterministically.

## Background / Rationale
Execution extras change operational behavior in ways that can be confusing without clear docs:
- multiple remote hosts
- prompt trimming/splitting
- two-message protocols

These features are optional, but when enabled they must be predictable and debuggable.

## Scope
### README.md
- Remote pool:
  - how to configure ORACLE_REMOTE_POOL (host:port list)
  - host selection policy + how to override
  - how busy interacts with host selection
- Prompt size controls:
  - size estimation: what is counted (bytes vs chars)
  - trim strategy (deterministic ordering)
  - split-bundle mode: the exact multi-message contract
- Operational guidance:
  - recommended flags/env for high-throughput usage
  - what artifacts/logs to consult when something goes wrong

### SPEC.md
- Document:
  - prompt sizing rules
  - split protocol schema
  - error taxonomy additions for size/trim/split failures (aligned with bd-3tj)

## Acceptance Criteria
- A user can enable remote pool + size controls and understand the behavior without reading source.
- Docs make failure modes + remediation obvious.

## Dependencies
- Depends on implementation + tests for these features.

title: Queue runner: sequential execution + busy integration + ledger/metrics updates
description:
## Objective
Implement the core queue runner that executes queued runs deterministically and safely.

## Background / Rationale
Queueing is only valuable if the runner is:
- crash-safe (queue state remains coherent)
- idempotent (restarts don't duplicate work)
- polite under busy conditions (wait/backoff instead of failing)
- observable (operators can tell what it's doing)

## Scope
### Core runner semantics
- Dequeue policy:
  - pick the next `queued` entry in a stable order (FIFO unless documented otherwise)
  - mark it `running` atomically before starting work (per bd-3fn event log)
- Execution:
  - invoke the same underlying run logic used by `apr run` (no divergent semantics)
  - always gate on lint (refuse to invoke Oracle if lint fails)
  - handle oracle busy:
    - wait/backoff/status-poll until available
    - record wait duration + attempts in queue state and in the run ledger
- Outcomes:
  - on success:
    - mark entry `done` and record output path + slug + timestamps
    - emit event `finish` with `ok:true`, `code:"ok"`, `exit_code:0`
  - on failure:
    - mark entry `failed` with `code` + `exit_code` + stderr digest/snippet
    - emit event `fail` (bd-3fn)
  - on cancel:
    - respect `canceled` entries (skip; preserve audit log)

### Concurrency + locking
- Ensure a single runner per workflow:
  - acquire a workflow-level lock before processing
  - clearly report lock contention (another runner active)

### Crash safety
- If APR crashes mid-run:
  - queue entry remains `running` with enough info to diagnose
  - restarts can either:
    - resume safely (if idempotency allows)
    - or mark stale running entries as failed with a clear reason (stable `code` per bd-3tj)

### Loop mode
- Support continuous loop mode (until queue empty), with a predictable exit code.

## Dependencies
- Depends on queue data model + atomic update strategy (bd-3fn).
- Depends on lint plumbing (bd-vlq) + error taxonomy (bd-3tj).
- Depends on busy handling (bd-2kd).
- Depends on run ledger (bd-1mt) so every queued run is auditable.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `apr queue run` can be left running and produces deterministic results.
- Queue state is coherent even across crashes/restarts.
- Runner never invokes Oracle when lint fails.

## Test coverage
- Covered by integration + E2E (mock oracle) tests (bd-3uu, bd-2tj).
- Tests must log:
  - queue transitions
  - busy waits
  - selected entry id
  - ledger paths
close_reason: Added core queue runner primitives: oldest queued selection, locked run-once start/finish/fail transitions, runner env handoff, and unit coverage

title: Docs: update README/SPEC for lint, manifest, queue, busy handling, and strict guards
description:
## Objective
Update APR documentation so users discover and correctly use the **core vNext** safety + provenance + orchestration features.

## Background / Rationale
Safety features are only useful if users understand:
- why APR refuses to run
- how to fix workflows quickly
- which override knobs exist (and when they are appropriate)

Core vNext is explicitly about making expensive Oracle runs harder to waste.

## Scope (core docs)
### README.md
- Document new/updated core commands:
  - `apr lint`
  - `apr doctor`
  - `apr workflow explain`
  - queue commands (`apr queue ...`) as part of core execution robustness
- Document prompt safety and strictness:
  - placeholder guards (e.g. `{{...}}` rejection)
  - strict mode (`--fail-on-warn` / `APR_FAIL_ON_WARN=1`)
  - explicit escape hatches (e.g. `APR_ALLOW_CURLY_PLACEHOLDERS=1`) and when they are appropriate
- Document provenance UX:
  - manifest behavior + how to preview it (`apr render --show-manifest`)
  - copying the exact resolved prompt (manifest+template)
  - ledger artifact location + what it records
- Document remote oracle basics (existing + core-relevant):
  - env vars
  - how busy/single-flight is handled

### SPEC.md
- Document the core contracts:
  - manifest format and semantics
  - ACK policy semantics
  - run ledger schema + versioning
  - error taxonomy:
    - robot JSON `.code` values (stable)
    - human exit-code meanings
    - human stderr tag `APR_ERROR_CODE=<code>` for fatal errors

## Explicitly out-of-scope (documented elsewhere)
These are valuable, but are tracked as separate docs tasks so they do not block closing the core doc bead:
- Operational extras docs: bd-1tk
- Execution extras docs (remote pool + oversize handling): bd-11p
- Hardening extras docs (confirm/model policy/redaction/secret scan): bd-1zu
- Safe templating UX/docs: bd-3uq

## Acceptance Criteria
- A new user can fix a failed lint/doctor error without reading source code.
- The docs make the "safe by default" philosophy obvious.

title: Queue commands: add/status/cancel/run (+ robot equivalents)
description:
## Objective
Implement the user-facing CLI surface for the persistent queue feature so operators can safely serialize many rounds without needing to understand internal queue storage.

## Background / Rationale
In real usage, APR runs are:
- expensive (browser automation)
- single-flight (oracle serve busy)
- frequently repeated (many iterative rounds)

A queue makes the workflow ergonomic and reliable:
- enqueue work quickly
- let APR serialize runs
- get deterministic, inspectable state

## Scope
### Commands (human)
- `apr queue add <round>`
  - Flags:
    - `--workflow <name>` (default: from config)
    - `--include-impl` (or equivalent) to match `apr run` behavior
    - optional `--slug <slug>` override (if supported by run)
  - Output:
    - prints the created queue entry id + summary (stderr)
- `apr queue status`
  - Shows:
    - queued count
    - currently-running entry (if any)
    - last N completed/failed entries (configurable)
  - Must be readable in both wide and narrow terminals.
- `apr queue cancel <id>`
  - Marks an entry as canceled (no destructive deletes; preserve audit trail).
- `apr queue run`
  - Processes the queue using the queue runner.
  - Flags:
    - `--once` (process a single entry and exit)
    - `--until-empty` (default; loop until queue drained)
    - `--max N` (optional safety cap)

### Commands (robot)
- Robot equivalents for automation:
  - `apr robot queue add/status/cancel/run`
  - JSON output uses stable `.code` values per bd-3tj.

### Behavioral requirements
- Queue commands must be safe-by-default:
  - no silent drops of entries
  - no destructive deletes
  - clear error messages when queue file is corrupted or locked
- Queue commands must integrate with the error contract:
  - consistent exit codes
  - consistent robot `.code` values and data shapes

## Dependencies
- Depends on queue data model (bd-3fn).
- Depends on queue runner implementation (bd-12b) so `apr queue run` is real.
- Depends on error taxonomy (bd-3tj).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Queue can be used without reading internal queue file format.
- A user can enqueue rounds and confidently observe progress/state from `apr queue status`.
- Robot mode can manage the queue without scraping stderr.

## Test coverage
- Covered by integration + E2E (mock oracle) tests (bd-3uu, bd-2tj).
- Tests must emit rich, timestamped logs.

title: Tests: code + exit-code contract (robot JSON + human stderr tags)
description:
## Status (2026-05-13): DONE — landed in commit 3b0f7d8

tests/integration/test_error_contract.bats provides a 16-test contract conformance harness:
- Robot JSON shape + .code + meta on stdout
- APR_ERROR_CODE=<code> tag on stderr
- Exit class per apr_exit_code_for_code mapping

Coverage: usage_error, not_configured, validation_failed, dependency_missing across apr robot {validate,run,unknown} and human apr run. Plus table-driven exit-class invariants.

One known gap pinned with skip + follow-up bead automated_plan_reviser_pro-19bh: human cmd_run placeholder branch doesn't route through apr_fail.

This bead cannot be moved to closed because parent epic automated_plan_reviser_pro-ufc is intentionally open; mark closed when ufc closes.

---

(original description preserved below)

## Objective
Add tests that enforce the stability of APR's error contract across commands:
- exit codes (human CLI)
- code field in robot JSON
- a short machine-readable stderr tag

title: Robot busy behavior: structured busy state vs enqueue policy
description:
## Objective
Define and implement deterministic `apr robot run` / `apr robot validate` behavior when Oracle is busy (single-flight), so automation can respond without brittle string matching.

## Background / Rationale
Robot mode is used by orchestrators. When Oracle is busy, the worst outcomes are:
- non-deterministic behavior (sometimes wait, sometimes fail)
- stringly-typed parsing of stderr

Robot busy behavior must be explicit, structured, and stable.

## Scope
### Robot busy policy (design)
Decide and document the default behavior when Oracle is busy:
- **Option A (default-recommended):** Return a structured busy failure immediately (`.code="busy"`).
- **Option B (opt-in):** Wait/backoff in robot mode up to a timeout.
- **Option C (opt-in):** Enqueue work (if queue features are enabled) and return `queued`.

This bead defines the behavior and how it is expressed in JSON + exit codes.

### JSON contract
When busy occurs, robot JSON must include:
- stable `.code` per bd-3tj (e.g. `busy`)
- data fields sufficient for automation, e.g.:
  - `busy: true`
  - `remote_host` (if applicable)
  - `retry_after_ms` (best-effort)
  - `policy` (error|wait|enqueue)
  - `queue_entry_id` when enqueue policy is used

### CLI/flag surface (if needed)
- Allow overriding busy policy in robot mode (flag or env) without changing defaults.
- Ensure policy is consistent across:
  - `apr robot run`
  - queue runner internals when invoked via robot wrappers

## Dependencies
- Depends on busy detection (bd-3pu).
- Depends on error taxonomy (bd-3tj).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Robots can handle busy using structured fields (no stderr scraping).
- Behavior is deterministic and documented.

## Test coverage
- Covered by integration + mock E2E tests (bd-3uu, bd-2tj).
close_reason: Structured robot busy contract: docs/schemas/robot-busy.md + lib/busy.sh apr_lib_busy_robot_data + apr robot_emit_busy + 15 tests. Default policy=error; wait/enqueue documented as opt-ins for bd-2kd / bd-12b. Robots can branch on .code='busy' and .data.signature without scraping stderr.

title: APR Tests/CI/Docs for Lint+Manifest+Queue Features
description:
## Objective
Ensure the new APR reliability/orchestration features are regression-proof via tests + CI, and discoverable via docs.

## Background / Rationale
APR is used in high-throughput, high-cost workflows (browser automation). A single obvious mistake (like unexpanded placeholders) can waste minutes and money. Reliability features must be enforced by tests.

APR is also environment-sensitive:
- Oracle local vs Oracle remote (`oracle serve`)
- single-flight busy behavior
- gum present/absent
- macOS vs Linux differences

## Scope
- **Fixture migration:** update existing test fixtures/workflows to be placeholder-free by default; keep one dedicated fixture that intentionally contains `{{...}}` to test the guard.
- **Unit tests (BATS):** prompt QC, lint validation, doc size policy, error taxonomy.
- **Integration tests (BATS):** busy/backoff behavior, queue sequencing, idempotency behavior.
- **E2E tests (mock Oracle):** full CLI journey across lint+manifest+ledger+busy+queue with timestamped logs.
- **Docs:** README/SPEC updates for lint/doctor/queue/manifest/ledger/overrides.
- **CI:** shellcheck + bats; explicit fixture asserting placeholder leaks are caught.

## Non-goals (for this epic)
- Real browser automation runs in CI.
- Live-remote oracle serve smoke tests.
  - Those remain valuable, but are tracked separately (bd-3a4) so they don't block core shipping.

## Logging requirements (non-negotiable)
- Every test writes a timestamped log file under `tests/logs/` with:
  - command invoked
  - env vars relevant to behavior
  - captured stdout/stderr
  - paths to any artifacts produced (round outputs, ledgers)

## Dependency note
Integrate with the existing testing infrastructure epic (automated_plan_reviser_pro-ufc) rather than duplicating it.

## Acceptance Criteria
- CI fails if placeholder leaks reappear.
- CI fails if robot/human `.code`/exit-code contracts regress.
- The mock-Oracle E2E suite validates end-to-end artifact behavior (manifest + ledger + queue).
- Docs clearly explain how to fix common failures and how to override strict guards intentionally.

title: Model/engine policy: enforce extended-reasoning defaults and require explicit overrides
description:
## Objective
Prevent accidental low-quality runs by enforcing a *lint-time policy* around model selection and browser thinking-time settings, while still allowing intentional experimentation via explicit overrides.

## Background / Rationale
APR is primarily designed for iterative spec refinement where model quality matters a lot. If a workflow accidentally targets a weaker model or uses non-extended thinking settings, the output is often bad but not obviously "broken". That leads to wasted cycles.

This bead makes APR nudge users toward the intended high-quality defaults.

## Scope
### 1) Add policy checks to `apr lint`
Inspect workflow config (oracle section) and emit warnings when settings are likely to degrade results.

Policy checks (examples; finalize allowlist/thresholds):
- Model strength:
  - warn if `oracle.model` looks like a non-pro / instant model (e.g. `gpt-5.2`, `gpt-5.2-instant`) when APR is configured for deep spec work
  - allow known-good values like `5.2 Thinking`, `gpt-5.2-pro`, etc.
- Thinking-time:
  - warn if `oracle.thinking_time` is missing
  - warn if it is set to a low value (anything below `heavy` by default)

The warnings must be:
- concrete (show the exact workflow file + line hit)
- actionable (show the recommended setting)

### 2) Explicit override mechanisms (no silent bypass)
Provide explicit, auditable ways to silence these warnings when users intentionally deviate:
- allow an env/flag override like:
  - `APR_ALLOW_NONPRO_MODELS=1`
  - `APR_ALLOW_LIGHT_THINKING=1`
